title: Understand: Equality, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness
description: Check your understanding of the principles of the American Revolution, specifically equality, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness, in this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Principles of the American Revolution: The Ideas and Events That Led to Change
Principles of the American Revolution: Equality, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness
Understand: Equality, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which document, based on the principles of the American Revolution, established the government of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Magna Carta
The Gettysburg Address
The Emancipation Proclamation
The Constitution

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the authors of the Declaration of Independence mean by “created equal”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All people naturally have certain rights that must be protected.
People are born free and equal, but the government determines their rights.
Those in the ruling class are superior to ordinary citizens.
Every person must follow the law, and no one is above it.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the authors of the Declaration of Independence mean by “liberty”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Freedom of the press
Freedom to establish a monarchy
Freedom from tyranny
Freedom from any kind of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the authors of the Declaration of Independence mean by *happiness* in the phrase “the pursuit of happiness”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Personal wealth
The public good
Freedom of speech
Elected representation
The sacrifice of private interests for the public good
The desire to constantly improve oneself
The goal of making as much money as possible
The flaws that every human possesses
When the government is subject to the will of the people
When there is a single head of government
When the government rules in a cruel way or with total control
When there is equality under the law for all people

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why was it important to the founders that people be virtuous?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because in a republic, power ultimately comes from the people
because the United States didn’t yet have a system of criminal courts
because they wanted to prove that Americans were superior to the British
because they were afraid that many people secretly supported the British

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement is true about republican governments in the 18th century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Almost every country in the world had a republican form of government.
Only European countries had republican governments.
Many countries were replacing republican governments with oligarchies.
Large republican governments did not exist after the fall of Rome.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the principles in the Declaration of Independence, what action can people take when they face a tyrannical government?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They can move somewhere else.
They can ask other monarchies to restore order.
They can call upon a higher power to influence leaders.
They can overthrow and replace the government.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Caroline Winterer, which activity would be an example of the founders’ idea of “the pursuit of happiness”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The right to choose one’s career as an adult
The ability to move to another state or country
The requirement to pay taxes to fund the military
The power to acquire wealth and property

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Caroline Winterer, what is an example of the founders’ idea of “liberty”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The freedom of movement within a country
The right to follow all laws passed by the government
The freedom to pay taxes to the government
The right to representation in government

assessment_items.question_content:
** In a republic, governmental power comes from the \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The king
The laws
The representatives
The people
